classes.subject: Mathematics
classes.part_or_section: Curriculum Structure and Question Paper Design

headings: Representation of rational numbers on the number line
Density of rational numbers and its proof
Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers

subtopics: nth term of AP and GP
Graphical method for pairs of equations

headings: Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
subtopics: Checking for right-angled triangles via coordinates

headings: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
subtopics: Linear pair theorem, vertically opposite angles
SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, AAS congruence conditions
Midpoint theorem for triangles and quadrilaterals

possible_chapters: Surface Area and Volume of Solids
subtopics: Generalisation vs Special case in formulas
Surface area/volume of Cuboids, Cubes, Cylinders, Cones, Pyramids, Spheres
Tree diagrams and tables for probability

formative_only_content.title: Continuous and Holistic Assessment
formative_only_content.source_text: Continuous and holistic assessment through projects, activities, and investigations forms an integral part of the learning process, moving beyond summative examinations.

practicals.title: Lab Practical
practicals.source_text: Lab activities to be done from the prescribed books (05 Marks)

practicals.title: Geometric Constructions and Models
practicals.source_text: Constructing a square with a given side as described in the Baudhayana's Sulbasutras; Construct the circumcircle and circumcentre of a triangle; Practice forming logical converses... Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

practicals.title: Square Root Spiral Construction
practicals.source_text: Construct the square root spiral. Apply computational thinking to represent rational and irrational numbers through algorithms and visual models.

practicals.title: Visualising Identities with Algebra Tiles
practicals.source_text: Visualising factorisation of quadratic expressions through algebra tiles and without using algebra tiles. Visualise algebraic identities using geometric models.

activities.title: Tower of Hanoi Puzzle
activities.source_text: Solve the Tower of Hanoi puzzle. Use computational thinking to identify patterns, write step-by-step rules.

activities.title: Fractals Analysis
activities.source_text: Analyse attributes of fractals using GP. Use two-dimensional representations of three-dimensional objects to visualise and solve problems.

activities.title: Floor Plan Modelling
activities.source_text: Represent a floor plan on a grid using coordinates. Apply computational thinking to model situations on the coordinate plane.

activities.title: Geometric Pattern Discovery
activities.source_text: Engage in drawing, measurement and paper manipulation activities to discover geometric patterns involving triangles and 4-gons.

activities.title: Data Analysis Projects
activities.source_text: Collect, organise, visualise and interpret data to answer a statistical investigative question. Use computational thinking strategies such as pattern recognition and simulation.

activities.title: Cultural Motifs Investigation
activities.source_text: Identify cultural motifs involving circles, for example, the Dharmachakra, Ashoka Chakra, Sudarshan Chakra.

projects.title: Mathematical Modelling of Daily Life
projects.source_text: Models daily-life phenomena and uses representations, such as graphs, tables, and equations to draw conclusions. Model linear growth and decay using linear polynomials.

projects.title: Historical Contributions Research
projects.source_text: Recognises the important contributions made by mathematicians (Indian and others) in the field of Mathematics. List historical approximations to π.

projects.title: Cross-Disciplinary Applications
projects.source_text: Applies mathematical knowledge and tools to analyse problems or situations across Science, Social Science, Visual Arts, Music, Vocational Education, and Sports.

marks_distribution: Question Paper Typology (External): Remembering/Understanding - 43 Marks, Applying - 19 Marks, Analysing/Evaluating/Creating - 18 Marks
excluded_or_non_assessed_content: None explicitly excluded in the provided text; all listed topics are part of the curriculum.
Note on SSA congruence condition: 'Explain why SSA is not, in general, a valid congruence condition' implies it is non-assessed as a proof method but assessed for understanding its limitations.
teacher_notes: Curriculum redesigned in alignment with NEP 2020 and NCF-SE 2023.
Prioritize deep conceptual understanding, logical reasoning, and competency-based education over rote learning.
Integrate Indian Knowledge System (e.g., Baudhayana's Sulbasutras, Brahmagupta, Aryabhata) with contemporary knowledge.
Emphasis on computational thinking: decomposing problems, algorithmic problem-solving, visualisation.
Use of real-life applications and experiential learning is critical.
Assessment includes continuous holistic assessment via projects, activities, and investigations.

Prescribed Books for Lab Practical: 1. Mathematics - Textbook for class IX (NCERT), 2. Guidelines for Mathematics Laboratory in Schools (CBSE), 3. Laboratory Manual - Mathematics (NCERT), 4. Mathematics exemplar problems (NCERT).
Time allocation for Question Paper Design: 3 Hours.
Internal Assessment components: Pen Paper Test and Multiple Assessment (10 Marks), Portfolio (5 Marks), Lab Practical (5 Marks).
